svg.title: dbOps Intelligence — from internal RCA tool to bank-wide and external product
svg.desc: Architecture showing how the existing Ollama/Gemma RCA system extends into a productized platform

g.text: What you already built — approved by Central Architecture Board
Ollama + Gemma, fully air-gapped, running on GDC/GKE

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the Ollama Gemma RCA system detect pipeline failures — what is the detection mechanism?')
sendPrompt('How does the anomaly detection system work on the data platform — what signals does it watch?')
sendPrompt('How does the LLM generate root cause analysis text from pipeline logs and metrics?')

g.text: Productization layer — what needs to be built
Transform single-team tool into multi-tenant platform

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do you build multi-tenancy into an Ollama-based LLM platform — namespace isolation, data separation?')
sendPrompt('What is a plugin SDK for a platform like dbOps — how do teams onboard custom data sources?')
sendPrompt('How would you build a self-service onboarding flow for bank teams to connect their pipelines to dbOps?')
sendPrompt('What observability and usage metrics matter for an internal platform product at a bank?')
sendPrompt('How many engineering teams at Deutsche Bank could benefit from automated RCA and anomaly detection?')
sendPrompt('What is the internal cost saving from automated root cause analysis at a bank with thousands of pipelines?')
sendPrompt('Which banks and financial institutions would pay for an air-gap compliant AIOps platform like dbOps?')
sendPrompt('How does licensing an internal AI platform externally work — what legal and IP structures does a bank use?')

svg.text: Already built + board approved + 0 dev cost to demonstrate = highest-credibility submission
You built this solo. You have bank-wide architecture approval. You are the platform owner.
Proposing productization = you become the product lead, not just the engineer who built it.
